summary:
The primary limitation preventing use of nitrous oxide alone as a G.A. agent lies in its inability to sustain appropriate oxygen levels required for extended surgical procedures. This critical deficiency makes it unsuitable when compared with other agents or mixtures designed specifically for safe induction and maintenance of deep sedation states where stable respiratory support including consistent O₂ supply are paramount considerations. \boxed{\text{Oxygen level maintenance}} was identified by Agent2 as the most pertinent issue from their initial response; this directly addresses how inadequate oxygen management restricts solo application of N<sub>2</sub>O under GA conditions despite potential benefits otherwise offered through such administration methods typically employed adjunctively rather than independently due precisely these physiological constraints highlighted here today!

formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 1 summarized): The primary limitation preventing use of nitrous oxide alone as a G.A. agent lies in its inability to sustain appropriate oxygen levels required for extended surgical procedures. This critical deficiency makes it unsuitable when compared with other agents or mixtures designed specifically for safe induction and maintenance of deep sedation states where stable respiratory support including consistent O₂ supply are paramount considerations. \boxed{\text{Oxygen level maintenance}} was identified by Agent2 as the most pertinent issue from their initial response; this directly addresses how inadequate oxygen management restricts solo application of N<sub>2</sub>O under GA conditions despite potential benefits otherwise offered through such administration methods typically employed adjunctively rather than independently due precisely these physiological constraints highlighted here today!
